caption:
Looking for things to do around Florida? Comment "Guide" and I'll send you my interactive Florida map and guide with over 1,000 spots to explore! I did the work for you so you can just hit the road without hours of research and stress!

Save this itinerary for your next trip to Venice, Florida!🌴

#veniceflorida #floridahiddengems #floridavacation #floridabeaches

transcription.text: Here's how to spend the perfect weekend getaway in Venice, Florida. And yes, you're going to want to steal this itinerary. Start off by flying into Sarasota International Airport and driving 30 minutes south to Venice or road tripping from wherever you are. And you're going to check into the Inn at the Beach, which is located right next to Venice Beach. You can literally spend your entire weekend on the island of Venice without driving anywhere. Only 0.3 miles away is historic downtown Venice, where you will wander the Venice Avenue walking path, pass the amazing banyan trees to downtown from your hotel or accommodation of choice. Next, you're going to explore historic downtown Venice. Venice is known as a city on the Gulf and is the most quaint and laid back beach town you'll find in Florida, in my opinion. Not only is it cute, but it's also very safe. So it's perfect for solo travelers. Grab a smoothie or other healthy breakfast at a local favorite Mission Cafe. Check out the historic Venice train depot, where the Ringling Brothers Circus train disembarked for its winter quarters beginning in 1960. You can take a free tour of the Ringling Brothers train car and train depot. And this is a very cool part of the Ringling Brothers Circus history that you should definitely check out. For lunch, grab the largest slice of pizza in Venice at the Soda Fountain. I love their 50s themed restaurant. And yes, a slice of pizza is the size of your head. Then grab an ice cream cone at the Venice Avenue Creamery. You can't go to the shark tooth capital of the world without going to the beach and hunting for shark's teeth. I always leave with a handful. For upscale dining on the beach, go to Finn's on Sharky's Beach for amazing seafood, sushi, and craft cocktails. Sit on the outside upper deck for these views.
